spec_match.rationale: Two real flex containers, one with `flex-wrap: wrap`, one with `flex-wrap: balance` — the new spec value is applied directly via CSS, no JS approximation.

interactive.rationale: Three working buttons: Shuffle tags, Narrow container, Widen container. The shuffle picks 20 random tags each click, narrow / wide change `max-width`. The before/after is visually obvious.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Blurb says `Toggle between wrap and balance and watch the lines settle into roughly equal lengths` — that is exactly what the page shows, side-by-side.

edge_cases.rationale: No feature-detection or `@supports (flex-wrap: balance)` branch — on a browser without support the user sees two identical lopsided clouds and no explanation. No imbalance score, no overflow case (one giant tag), no two-line vs three-line crossover.

design_system.rationale: Variables only, the `[aria-pressed=true]` toggle styling sets background AND color together, no static-card pattern, contrast pairs are AA.

openQuestions.title: Add a CSS.supports() probe for `flex-wrap: balance`
openQuestions.detail: On a browser without the new value the two clouds render identically and the visitor cannot tell whether the demo is broken or the browser is old. Add a small `Your browser: supports / does not support flex-wrap: balance` badge driven by `CSS.supports('flex-wrap', 'balance')`, and if unsupported, swap one cloud to a JS-shimmed balanced layout so the contrast is still visible.

openQuestions.title: Show a numeric imbalance score
openQuestions.detail: The sibling `chip-editor` and `line-load-visualizer` concepts compute `max(lineCount) - min(lineCount)` per cloud as an imbalance score. Bring that to the canonical headline demo too — the number gives authors a concrete metric for why balance helps.

openQuestions.title: Demonstrate the stranded-orphan case explicitly
openQuestions.detail: The strongest visual win for `balance` is the case where `wrap` leaves a single chip on its own line. The current shuffle is random so it may not hit that case on every reload. Add a `Force orphan` preset that picks a tag set guaranteed to leave one chip stranded with `wrap` — then balance rescues it.

openQuestions.title: Show what happens with one oversize child
openQuestions.detail: Edge case: a tag wider than the container. With `wrap` it occupies its own line; with `balance` the algorithm's behaviour around an item that cannot itself shrink should be documented. Add an `add huge tag` button and a sentence describing the expected layout.
